title: Ouratea gillyana
intro: Ouratea gillyana, also known as "Sapo de Ouratea" or "Sapo de la Montaña", is a plant species in the family Ochnaceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Ouratea gillyana include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Ouratea gillyana include infusions, decoctions, and topical creams, which are used to treat a range of health issues, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and potential side effects.
